title: For Macron, the legalization of aid in dying meant a long personal journey
subhead: After doubting the need for new legislation, fearing he might upset religious leaders and concerned about dividing French society, the president eventually resolved to support the legislation, under pressure from MPs and public opinion.

subhead: Environment Minister Monique Barbut said the drought was 'exceptionally' early France, which is sweltering under its third heatwave of the summer.

title: US targets ICC in new offensive
subhead: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reopened a diplomatic campaign on Monday aimed at dismantling the ICC by combining sanctions, putting pressure on member states and seeking allies among major powers hostile to the institution.

title: Spain picks apart Mbappé's powerless France in World Cup semi-final
subhead: French captain Kylian Mbappé and his teammates fell to a Spanish side that outclassed them in every aspect of the game.

title: Judge of 2016 Nice terror attack trial reflects on 'abominable' case, 10 years after
subhead: Laurent Raviot presided over the Paris special criminal court that held the trial of the attack in Nice that killed 86 people on July 14, 2016. On the 10th anniversary of the attack, he granted Le Monde his first interview, speaking with unusual candor.

title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

title: How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
subhead: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.
